title: senecio sumarae
intro: Senecio sumarae, also known as Giant Ragwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula. The plant contains a range of bioactive compounds, including pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been found to have medicinal properties. These compounds have been traditionally used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, offering potential health benefits. Senecio sumarae can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
